text: I feel a sense of quiet unease as I move through these dimly-lit, almost deserted halls. The interplay of darkness and artificial light brings an awareness of my own presence in this space, as if I am both intruder and observer. The unfamiliar silence, punctuated by the glow of emergency lights and the cryptic message on the wall, invokes a certain existential weight—what purpose do these spaces serve without those who inhabit them? Here, I am reminded of the tension between potential and emptiness, between innovation and abandonment.
